Title: Transcriptomic characterization of two major Fusarium resistance quantitative trait loci (QTLs), Fhb1 and Qfhs.ifa-5A, identifies novel candidate genes

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type 1 nonspecific lipid transfer protein; PR-14 class LTP
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly associated with Qfhs.ifa-5A resistance; constitutively much more abundant in resistant lines and proposed to promote Fusarium resistance and lower DON accumulation indirectly
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ta.1282.4.S1_at was the most strongly constitutively enriched transcript in lines carrying Qfhs.ifa-5A, showing roughly 50-70-fold higher abundance. 2. The probe set corresponds to an LTP identical to the TaLTP9.4a gene model. 3. Mapping placed the gene on the pericentromeric region of chromosome 5AL, within the Qfhs.ifa-5A confidence interval, making it a major candidate gene.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: DON is a virulence toxin; reduced DON burden and increased detoxification are associated with safer grain and Fusarium resistance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively high in resistant Qfhs.ifa-5A background across 8, 24 and 72 hai
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively highly abundant in mock and infected spike tissues of NIL3 carrying Qfhs.ifa-5A
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum inoculation context; constitutive expression independent of infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix transcript profiling; RT-qPCR validation of representative transcripts; chromosome mapping with nulli-tetra and deletion lines; resequencing of parental alleles
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix Wheat Genome Array; PLEXdb accession TA41
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes this LTP as the obvious Qfhs.ifa-5A candidate. The mechanism may involve preformed barrier function, antifungal activity, cutin deposition, signaling, or toxin resistance. Authors caution that PR-14 LTPs can be food allergens.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential food allergenicity risk if deployed through overexpression, as many PR-14 LTPs are allergenic
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value candidate for breeding and functional validation; suitable for marker-assisted selection or transgenic/editing approaches, but allergenicity and direct causal validation in wheat should be assessed.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutively more abundant in the resistant Qfhs.ifa-5A background and suggested to participate in trichothecene detoxification/transport
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. An MDR-like ABC transporter transcript was constitutively more abundant in lines carrying Qfhs.ifa-5A. 2. The authors classify it as putative trichothecene detoxification and transport related. 3. It is a candidate contributor to lower toxin burden, though not functionally validated here.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence toxin whose detoxification/efflux contributes to Fusarium resistance and grain safety
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively higher in NIL3 versus NIL4 across 8, 24 and 72 hai
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Basally enriched in Qfhs.ifa-5A-resistant wheat lines
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix Wheat Genome Array; PLEXdb accession TA41
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is specifically highlighted as putative trichothecene detoxification and transport; direct transport activity was not tested.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for combinatorial stacking with detoxification genes, but requires direct functional validation before deployment.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to deoxynivalenol (DON), forming a DON glucoside detoxification product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxified conjugated form of DON associated with Fusarium resistance and reduced toxin damage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers weak resistance to DON; candidate Fhb1-associated detoxification gene likely contributing weakly to D3G formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaUGT12887 was identified as an Fhb1-associated transcript, especially in the genetic background also carrying Qfhs.ifa-5A. 2. The complete cDNA/ORF was reconstructed and mapped to chromosome 5BL. 3. Heterologous expression in toxin-sensitive Saccharomyces cerevisiae conferred DON resistance, but much weaker than barley HvUGT13248.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen responsive; higher induction in NIL1 versus NIL3, indicating Fhb1-associated induction when basal resistance is high
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Fusarium graminearum inoculation, mainly evident in wheat NIL carrying both Fhb1 and Qfhs.ifa-5A
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum inoculation; DON exposure in yeast assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics (Affymetrix array); gene model reconstruction; chromosome mapping; heterologous overexpression in yeast; DON resistance spotting assay; immunoblot validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix Wheat Genome Array; PLEXdb accession TA41
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Probe sets TaAffx.111585.1.S1_at and Ta.12887.1.S1_at originated from the same gene. Activity against DON was much weaker than HvUGT13248, suggesting TaUGT12887 may be DON responsive but not the primary detoxification enzyme in wheat.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a candidate marker/gene for stacking with stronger DON-detoxifying genes, but alone is unlikely to provide strong resistance; paralogue discovery and functional testing are recommended.
